rulings.Case Title: CHARLES AIKINS JR ET AL VS. ANCHOR DARLING VALVE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 02, 2024, in Department 301, Line 2. (1 OF 2) DEFENDANT CONTINENTAL MARITIME OF SAN FRANCISCO, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (1) Defendant Continental Maritime of San Francisco Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant. Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of causation for the following reasons. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) First, under California Code of Civil Procedure sec. 437c(f)(1), "A motion for summary adjudication shall be granted only if it completely disposes of a cause of action, an affirmative defense, a claim for damages, or an issue of duty." Defendant's motion does not seek to completely dispose of any claim. Instead, the motion merely seeks to limit the jury's assessment of damages. Defendant has not met this threshold with regard to their Motion for Summary Adjudication. Defendant supports its Motion for Summary Judgment with limited discovery responses, but has not shown that it propounded special discovery. (See Defendant's Exhibit C.) The "Certificate of Death'' offered by Defendants does not completely foreclose asbestos-related disease as a cause of Decedent's death. (See Defendant's Exhibit B.) Similarly, the articles of incorporation do not meet the burden of showing no possible causation by Defendant. (See Defendant's Exhibits E, F, and G.) The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on July 2, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost.

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES AIKINS JR ET AL VS. ANCHOR DARLING VALVE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 02, 2024, in Department 301, Line 2. (2 OF 2) DEFENDANT BAE SYSTEMS SAN DIEGO SHIP REPAIR INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (2) Defendant BAE System's Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative Summary Adjudication, is DENIED. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant. Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation and that there is no triable issue of fact. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant contends that Plaintiffs failed to show any link between BAE Systems and Decedent's injury because current discovery lacks an evidentiary link between Defendant and Decedent. But the Court finds that summary judgment cannot be granted where there was limited discovery, and "[a] motion for summary judgment is not a mechanism for rewarding limited discovery . . . ." (Weber v. John Crane, Inc., 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1442.) Defendant's special interrogatories did not seek all facts, all documents, and all witnesses; it merely sought information about the ships' names, dates, and the scope of Decedent's work on those ships. (Defense Exhibit C at 2-3.) Defendant has not deposed the witnesses Plaintiffs offered in its responses to general interrogatories. As such, Defendant's motion fails to address the full breadth of Plaintiffs' claims and fails to sustain its burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence to support their claims. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on July 2, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT MOORE SR. ET AL VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 02, 2024, in Department 301, Line 3. PLAINTIFF ALBERT MOORE, GLORIA MOORE Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery cutoff is GRANTED under CCP 36(a). No opposition filed. 1. The trial date is October 21, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206. a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is October 29, 2024. b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20. 2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is October 15, 2024. a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation. c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing. 3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days. a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter. 4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. 5. The fact discovery cut-off date is October 4, 2024. 6. The expert discovery cut-off date is October 18, 2024. 7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than August 2, 2024. a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission. b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. 8. No later than July 16, 2024: a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant. b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses. c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. (Tentative ruling continues in Part 2 of 2.)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN JOHNSON VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 02, 2024, in Department 301, Line 4. PLAINTIFF NORMAN JOHNSON Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery cutoff is DENIED without prejudice. The Court does not find at this time that the moving papers and counsel's declaration support a finding that Plaintiff's prognosis is such that preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing his interests in the litigation. No opposition filed. Plaintiff may re-file their motion should circumstances change. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.
